notes: Session-end's user-observable signal: the session row disappears within the expected TTL, and its state immediately before disappearing is ready. Pi supports only the clean-exit teardown variant. RE-RECORDED IN SCRIPT MODE (was PROMPT mode, which validated 1/5): the interactive pi REPL runs one normal turn (send + wait_turn), then the driver kills the tmux session so pi exits NATURALLY at script end — that natural process exit after the final ready IS the modeled clean-exit teardown. The pi interactive driver exposes send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep only (no exit_clean/sigkill keystroke), so SIGKILL-idle (variant 2) and SIGKILL-mid-turn (variant 3) stay out of scope; natural teardown is the single correctly-modeled variant. WHY the prompt-mode recording drifted (concerns C6 + C7): (C6) headless `pi --print` exits very fast at turn completion, so the post-turn settle the spec asserts was truncated; (C7) the live REPL emits a transient proc-<PID> pre-session born ready at launch, and the old spec pinned every turn phase + pid_bind + teardown to session_birth via same_session_as — but session_birth (which only asserts 'a session appears in ready within 1s of launch') legitimately matched that proc-<PID> pre-session, so the whole same_session_as chain bound to the wrong (pre-)session and cascaded to failure. C7 WORKAROUND (the same anchor-to-start fix that took multi-turn-conversation from 1/9 to 9/9, and interrupted-turn to 5/5): turn_start anchors to recording start with NO session pin so the matcher picks the FIRST working in the whole recording, which is necessarily the UUID rollout session (the pre-session never enters working); session_birth stays unpinned (it may match either row); every later phase — turn_end, pid_bind, and the load-bearing teardown — then pins to turn_start's session via same_session_as so a co-occurring proc-<PID> pre-session can never satisfy them. HOW runs differ: exact assistant wording, per-turn token counts, and sub-second timing vary (model-output variance); pi may emit additional mid-turn assistant bursts. Stable structurally: one ready→working→ready arc on one UUID rollout session that never enters waiting, a single process_exited on that rollout session AFTER the final ready, and a final ready state.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of REPL launch
User prompt → working (binds the rollout session; the proc-<PID> pre-session never enters working)
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
PID bound to the rollout session

REPL torn down once after the turn → pi exits naturally → a single process_exited on the rollout session after the final ready; the session row disappears and its state immediately before disappearing is ready
